findings.name: Excessive users with Procurement Administrator role
findings.description: 18 users hold the Procurement Administrator role which grants full control over sourcing events, contracts, and supplier management. Recommended maximum is 5.
findings.recommendation: Review and reduce Procurement Administrator assignments. Create granular custom roles for category-specific procurement activities.

findings.name: Dormant user accounts with active permissions
findings.description: 34 user accounts have not logged in for over 90 days but retain active roles including Contract Manager, Invoice Approver, and Sourcing Project Owner.
findings.recommendation: Implement automated account deactivation after 90 days of inactivity. Remove all role assignments from dormant accounts immediately.

findings.name: Separation of duties violation in procurement workflow
findings.description: 5 users simultaneously hold Requisition Creator and Approval Manager roles, allowing them to create and approve their own purchase requests without independent review.
findings.recommendation: Implement SoD policies that prevent users from holding conflicting roles. Configure approval chains requiring a different approver for self-created requisitions.

findings.name: Service accounts with interactive login capability
findings.description: 3 integration service accounts (svc_sap_erp, svc_fieldglass, svc_analytics) can perform interactive login. Service accounts should be restricted to API-only access.
findings.recommendation: Disable interactive login for all service accounts. Restrict authentication to OAuth client credentials grant or certificate-based auth.

findings.name: SAML SSO not enforced for all users
findings.description: SAML SSO is configured but not enforced. 23% of users can authenticate with local Ariba credentials bypassing corporate IdP policies including MFA.
findings.recommendation: Enforce SAML SSO for all non-service accounts. Disable local authentication fallback. Ensure the IdP enforces MFA for Ariba access.

findings.name: Password policy allows weak passwords
findings.description: Local password policy requires only 8 characters with no complexity requirements. This applies to accounts not using SSO and emergency access accounts.
findings.recommendation: Set minimum password length to 14 characters. Require uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters. Enable password history (12 passwords).

findings.name: Session timeout exceeds 60 minutes
findings.description: User session idle timeout is set to 120 minutes. Extended sessions on shared or public devices increase risk of session hijacking.
findings.recommendation: Reduce session idle timeout to 30 minutes. Implement absolute session timeout of 8 hours regardless of activity.

findings.name: API keys without rotation policy
findings.description: 7 Ariba API application keys have not been rotated in over 180 days. Long-lived API keys increase the window of exposure if compromised.
findings.recommendation: Implement a 90-day API key rotation policy. Use OAuth 2.0 with short-lived tokens instead of static API keys where possible.

findings.name: API rate limiting not configured
findings.description: No rate limiting is configured on the Ariba API endpoints. This allows potential abuse through excessive API calls for data extraction.
findings.recommendation: Configure API rate limits per application key. Set thresholds for requests per minute and daily quotas. Implement exponential backoff requirements.

findings.name: API integration using deprecated authentication method
findings.description: 2 API integrations (ERP-Sync, Legacy-Reports) use the deprecated shared secret authentication. This method does not support token expiration or scoping.
findings.recommendation: Migrate all API integrations to OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow. Decommission shared secret authentication method.

findings.name: Purchase order approval thresholds not tiered
findings.description: A single approval level is configured for all purchase orders regardless of amount. POs up to $500,000 require only one manager approval.
findings.recommendation: Implement tiered approval workflows: <$10K single approval, $10K-$100K two-level, >$100K three-level with VP sign-off.

findings.name: Emergency purchase order bypass used excessively
findings.description: Emergency PO bypass was used 47 times in the last 30 days, representing 12% of all purchase orders. Emergency bypass skips standard approval workflows.
findings.recommendation: Review and restrict emergency PO bypass authorization. Implement post-facto review process. Set monthly threshold alerts for bypass usage.

findings.name: Contract expiration notifications not configured
findings.description: Automatic notifications for expiring contracts are not enabled. 15 contracts with a combined value of $2.3M expired without renewal or renegotiation.
findings.recommendation: Enable contract expiration notifications at 90, 60, and 30 days before expiry. Assign contract owners responsible for renewal workflows.

findings.name: Maverick spending detection not enabled
findings.description: No rules are configured to detect and flag off-contract or maverick spending. Purchases outside contracted suppliers are not identified for review.
findings.recommendation: Configure guided buying rules to flag off-contract purchases. Implement preferred supplier enforcement and compliance reporting.

findings.name: Sensitive supplier data accessible without field-level security
findings.description: Supplier bank account details, tax IDs, and contact information are visible to all users with Supplier Manager role (42 users) without field-level restrictions.
findings.recommendation: Implement field-level security for sensitive supplier data. Restrict bank account and tax ID visibility to Finance and Supplier Master Data roles only.

findings.name: Data export controls not restricted
findings.description: Bulk data export functionality is available to 67 users. Large exports of procurement data, pricing, and supplier information are not logged or restricted.
findings.recommendation: Restrict bulk export to authorized roles. Implement export logging and alerting. Set maximum record limits for data exports.

findings.name: Document attachments not scanned for malware
findings.description: Supplier-uploaded documents (invoices, proposals, certificates) are not scanned for malware before being stored or accessed by internal users.
findings.recommendation: Enable document scanning integration for all uploaded attachments. Quarantine suspicious files and notify the security team.

findings.name: ERP integration credentials stored in plaintext
findings.description: SAP ERP integration connection uses plaintext credentials in the integration configuration. These credentials grant write access to the ERP procurement module.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to certificate-based authentication for ERP integration. Store any required credentials in a secrets management vault.

findings.name: Integration middleware using unencrypted channel
findings.description: The Dell Boomi integration middleware communicates with Ariba over HTTP (not HTTPS) on 2 of 5 integration flows, exposing procurement data in transit.
findings.recommendation: Enforce TLS 1.2+ for all integration channels. Update middleware configuration to use HTTPS endpoints exclusively.

findings.name: Third-party integration with excessive data access
findings.description: Analytics integration (PowerBI-Connector) has read access to all procurement data including supplier financials. Scope exceeds reporting requirements.
findings.recommendation: Scope integration data access to specific data views needed for reporting. Remove access to sensitive supplier financial data.

findings.name: Supplier self-registration without verification workflow
findings.description: Supplier self-registration portal does not require identity verification or compliance checks before granting access to the sourcing network.
findings.recommendation: Implement a supplier verification workflow including business registration validation, compliance checks, and manual approval before activation.

findings.name: Supplier bank account changes not requiring dual approval
findings.description: Changes to supplier bank account details require only single approval. This is a primary vector for Business Email Compromise (BEC) payment fraud.
findings.recommendation: Implement dual approval for all supplier bank account changes. Require out-of-band verification with a known supplier contact before processing.

findings.name: Duplicate supplier records detected
findings.description: 23 potential duplicate supplier records detected based on matching tax IDs and similar company names. Duplicates can be used to circumvent spend controls.
findings.recommendation: Run supplier deduplication. Enable automatic duplicate detection rules for new supplier registrations. Merge confirmed duplicate records.

findings.name: Audit logging not covering all security events
findings.description: Audit logging is enabled but does not capture: role assignment changes, approval delegation modifications, or API key generation events.
findings.recommendation: Enable comprehensive audit logging for all security-relevant events. Configure log export to SIEM for correlation and alerting.

findings.name: No periodic access certification process
findings.description: No formal access certification or recertification process is in place. User roles and permissions are not reviewed on a scheduled basis.
findings.recommendation: Implement quarterly access certification campaigns. Require managers to review and attest to their reports' access levels.

findings.name: Compliance reports not generated automatically
findings.description: Compliance and spend reports are generated manually on an ad-hoc basis. There is no automated compliance reporting schedule.
findings.recommendation: Configure automated weekly compliance reports covering: off-contract spend, approval bypasses, and SoD violations. Distribute to compliance team.

findings.name: Test realm configuration replicated to production
findings.description: 3 configuration settings replicated from the test realm to production appear to be test configurations including relaxed validation rules.
findings.recommendation: Review all configurations migrated from test to production. Remove test-specific settings and enforce production-grade validation rules.

findings.name: Unused integration endpoints still active
findings.description: 5 integration endpoints are configured but have had zero traffic for over 180 days. Unused endpoints expand the attack surface.
findings.recommendation: Decommission unused integration endpoints. Implement periodic review of active integrations. Disable endpoints with no traffic for 90+ days.
